patterns.text: make a script executable
patterns.explanation: Adds execute permission for owner, group, and others

patterns.text: set read and write for owner only, no access for anyone else
patterns.explanation: Sets permissions to rw------- protecting the file from other users

patterns.text: make a directory and its contents readable by everyone
patterns.explanation: Recursively adds read permission for all users on every file and directory

patterns.text: remove write permission for group and others
patterns.explanation: Strips write access from group and other users

patterns.text: set standard web directory permissions
patterns.explanation: Owner can read/write/execute, everyone else can read and execute

patterns.text: set the setuid bit on a program
patterns.explanation: Allows the program to run with the file owner's privileges

patterns.text: change the owner of a file
patterns.explanation: Sets alice as the owner of report.pdf

patterns.text: change owner and group of a file at once
patterns.explanation: Sets both owner and group to www-data

patterns.text: recursively change ownership of a directory tree
patterns.explanation: Changes owner and group of /opt/app and everything inside it

patterns.text: change only the group of a file
patterns.explanation: Changes the group to developers without modifying the owner

patterns.text: change ownership of a symbolic link itself
patterns.explanation: Changes the owner of the symlink rather than the file it points to

commands.name: chgrp
commands.description: Change the group ownership of a file

patterns.text: change the group of a file
patterns.explanation: Sets the group of project.conf to developers

patterns.text: recursively change group for an entire directory
patterns.explanation: Changes the group to staff for all files under /shared/docs

patterns.text: change group of a symlink without following it
patterns.explanation: Changes the group of the symbolic link itself

patterns.text: change group and show what was modified
patterns.explanation: Changes the group of all PowerPoint files and reports each change

commands.name: umask
commands.description: Set the default permission mask for newly created files

patterns.text: check the current umask value
patterns.explanation: Displays the current permission mask in octal notation

patterns.text: set a restrictive umask so new files are private
patterns.explanation: New files will be accessible only by the owner (no group/other access)

patterns.text: show the current umask in symbolic format
patterns.explanation: Displays the effective permissions for new files in human-readable form

patterns.text: set umask so new files are readable by group members
patterns.explanation: Owner gets full access, group gets read/execute, others get nothing

patterns.text: set a permissive umask for shared development work
patterns.explanation: New files will be writable by the group, read-only for others

commands.name: sudo
commands.description: Execute a command as another user, typically root

patterns.text: run a command as root
patterns.explanation: Executes apt update with superuser privileges

patterns.text: run a command as a different user
patterns.explanation: Opens PostgreSQL shell as the postgres user

patterns.text: open a root shell
patterns.explanation: Starts an interactive login shell as root

patterns.text: edit a system file safely with sudo
patterns.explanation: Opens the file in your editor with root write access using sudoedit

patterns.text: check what sudo commands the current user is allowed to run
patterns.explanation: Lists the allowed and forbidden commands for the invoking user

patterns.text: run a command preserving the user's environment variables
patterns.explanation: Executes the script as root while keeping the current environment

patterns.text: switch to the root user
patterns.explanation: Opens a login shell as root with root's environment

patterns.text: switch to another user account
patterns.explanation: Switches to alice's account with a full login shell

patterns.text: run a single command as another user
patterns.explanation: Executes whoami as alice without starting an interactive shell

patterns.text: switch user preserving the current environment
patterns.explanation: Switches to alice but keeps the current working directory and environment

commands.name: visudo
commands.description: Safely edit the sudoers file with syntax checking

patterns.text: edit the sudoers file safely
patterns.explanation: Opens the sudoers file in an editor and validates syntax before saving

patterns.text: edit a specific sudoers drop-in file
patterns.explanation: Edits a separate sudoers file fragment with validation

patterns.text: check the sudoers file for syntax errors without editing
patterns.explanation: Validates the sudoers file and reports any parse errors

patterns.text: check a specific sudoers file for syntax errors
patterns.explanation: Validates only the specified sudoers fragment file

commands.name: setfacl
commands.description: Set file access control lists for fine-grained permissions

patterns.text: grant a specific user read access to a file
patterns.explanation: Adds an ACL entry giving user bob read permission on report.csv

patterns.text: give a group read and write access to a directory
patterns.explanation: Adds an ACL entry granting the devteam group read/write access

patterns.text: set default ACL so new files inherit permissions
patterns.explanation: New files created in the directory will automatically have devteam read/write access

patterns.text: remove all ACL entries from a file
patterns.explanation: Strips all extended ACL entries, leaving only standard permissions

patterns.text: recursively apply ACL permissions to a directory tree
patterns.explanation: Grants alice read and execute access to all files in the directory tree

patterns.text: view the ACL permissions on a file
patterns.explanation: Shows the full access control list including owner, group, and extended ACLs

patterns.text: check ACLs on multiple files at once
patterns.explanation: Displays ACLs for every file in the project directory

patterns.text: show only the effective permissions in the ACL
patterns.explanation: Displays the ACL with effective rights mask applied

patterns.text: recursively show ACLs for a directory tree
patterns.explanation: Lists ACLs for every file and subdirectory under the project folder

commands.name: chattr
commands.description: Change file attributes on a Linux file system

patterns.text: make a file immutable so it cannot be modified or deleted
patterns.explanation: Sets the immutable flag preventing any changes including by root

patterns.text: make a file append-only for logging
patterns.explanation: Only allows appending to the file, preventing modification of existing content

patterns.text: remove the immutable attribute from a file
patterns.explanation: Clears the immutable flag so the file can be modified again

patterns.text: recursively set the no-dump attribute on a directory
patterns.explanation: Marks all files in the cache directory to be excluded from dumps

commands.name: lsattr
commands.description: List file attributes on a Linux file system

patterns.text: show the special attributes of a file
patterns.explanation: Displays ext2/ext3/ext4 attributes like immutable or append-only

patterns.text: list attributes for all files in a directory
patterns.explanation: Shows the attributes for every file in the /etc directory

patterns.text: recursively list attributes for a directory tree
patterns.explanation: Shows attributes for all files and subdirectories recursively

patterns.text: show attributes of directories themselves rather than their contents
patterns.explanation: Displays attributes of the directory entry itself

patterns.text: change your own password
patterns.explanation: Prompts for the current password then asks for a new one

patterns.text: change another user's password as root
patterns.explanation: Sets a new password for alice without requiring the old one

patterns.text: lock a user account to prevent login
patterns.explanation: Disables password authentication for bob's account

patterns.text: unlock a previously locked user account
patterns.explanation: Re-enables password authentication for bob's account

patterns.text: force a user to change their password at next login
patterns.explanation: Expires alice's password so she must set a new one on next login

patterns.text: check the password status of a user account
patterns.explanation: Shows whether the account is locked, the last change date, and expiry info

patterns.text: create a new user with a home directory
patterns.explanation: Creates user alice with a home directory and bash as the default shell

patterns.text: create a system user for running a service
patterns.explanation: Creates a system account that cannot log in interactively

patterns.text: create a user and add them to supplementary groups
patterns.explanation: Creates alice with membership in the sudo and docker groups

patterns.text: create a user with a specific home directory and UID
patterns.explanation: Creates the deploy user with a custom home directory and user ID

patterns.text: create a user with an expiration date
patterns.explanation: Creates a user account that automatically expires at year end

patterns.text: delete a user account
patterns.explanation: Removes the user bob but leaves their home directory intact

patterns.text: delete a user and remove their home directory
patterns.explanation: Removes bob's account along with their home directory and mail spool

patterns.text: force delete a user even if they are currently logged in
patterns.explanation: Forces removal of the account even if the user has active processes

patterns.text: delete a user and clean up all their files
patterns.explanation: Forcibly removes the user, their home directory, and their mail spool

patterns.text: add a user to an additional group
patterns.explanation: Appends alice to the docker group without removing other group memberships

patterns.text: change a user's login shell
patterns.explanation: Sets zsh as alice's default login shell

patterns.text: lock a user account to prevent login
patterns.explanation: Locks bob's password so the account cannot be used for login

patterns.text: change a user's home directory and move their files
patterns.explanation: Moves alice's home directory and its contents to the new location

patterns.text: rename a user account
patterns.explanation: Changes the login name from oldname to newname

patterns.text: create a new group
patterns.explanation: Creates a new group named developers with an auto-assigned GID

patterns.text: create a group with a specific GID
patterns.explanation: Creates the webteam group with GID 1500

patterns.text: create a system group for a service
patterns.explanation: Creates a system group with a GID in the system range

patterns.text: force create a group even if the name already exists as a user
patterns.explanation: Creates the group or exits successfully if it already exists

patterns.text: delete a group
patterns.explanation: Removes the group oldteam from the system

patterns.text: force delete a group even if users have it as a supplementary group
patterns.explanation: Forcibly removes the group regardless of existing members

patterns.text: check if a group exists before deleting it
patterns.command: getent group oldteam && sudo groupdel oldteam
patterns.explanation: Only attempts deletion if the group exists, avoiding errors

patterns.text: remove a group and verify it was deleted
patterns.explanation: Deletes the group and confirms it no longer appears in /etc/group

patterns.text: rename a group
patterns.explanation: Changes the group name from oldname to newname

patterns.text: change a group's GID
patterns.explanation: Changes the GID of the developers group to 2000

patterns.text: set a password on a group
patterns.explanation: Sets an encrypted password that non-members must enter to use newgrp

patterns.text: rename a group and change its GID at the same time
patterns.explanation: Renames devops to engineering and changes its GID to 3000

commands.name: newgrp
commands.description: Log in to a new group, changing the current group ID

patterns.text: switch your active group for the current session
patterns.explanation: Changes the effective group to developers so new files belong to that group

patterns.text: switch to a group with a fresh login environment
patterns.explanation: Changes to the docker group and reinitializes the shell environment

patterns.text: temporarily work with docker group permissions after being added
patterns.explanation: Activates docker group membership without needing to log out and back in

patterns.text: reset to your default primary group
patterns.explanation: Returns your effective group back to your primary login group

commands.name: chpasswd
commands.description: Update passwords in batch from standard input

patterns.text: set passwords for multiple users at once
patterns.explanation: Updates passwords for alice and bob in one batch operation

patterns.text: set a password from a file containing user:password pairs
patterns.explanation: Reads username:password pairs from the file and applies them

patterns.text: set a password using an encrypted hash
patterns.explanation: Sets the password from a pre-encrypted hash rather than plaintext

patterns.text: bulk reset passwords using SHA-512 encryption
patterns.explanation: Sets bob's password and encrypts it with SHA-512

commands.name: adduser
commands.description: Add a user to the system interactively with sensible defaults

patterns.text: create a new user interactively with all prompts
patterns.explanation: Walks through creating a user with home directory, password, and user info

patterns.text: add an existing user to a group
patterns.explanation: Adds alice to the docker group

patterns.text: create a system user for running a service
patterns.explanation: Creates a system user without a home directory or login shell

patterns.text: create a user with a specific home directory
patterns.explanation: Creates the deploy user with /opt/deploy as the home directory

patterns.text: create a user non-interactively for scripting
patterns.explanation: Creates user ci-runner with no password and no interactive prompts
